caption: The video appears to be a presentation on the topic of "OPS-5G TA3 – Secure Slices," focusing on mitigating new attack surfaces introduced by virtualization and slicing in 5G networks. The presenter, visible in a small inset window, explains the objective of securing different network slices, such as eMBB (Enhanced Mobile Broadband), URLLC (Ultra-Reliable Low Latency Communications), and mMTC (Massive Machine Type Communications). The slide illustrates the flow of data through various components like 5G gNBs, transport networks, edge DCs, local DCs, and central DCs for each type of slice. The presentation is associated with USC Viterbi School of Engineering and is part of an initiative involving The Linux Foundation, LF Networking, and LF Edge, emphasizing the importance of secure and efficient network slicing in 5G technology.
